CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INT PRIMARY KEY, event_date DATE);
INSERT INTO test_dates (id, event_date) VALUES (1, CURRENT_DATE);
INSERT INTO test_dates (id, event_date) VALUES (2, CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 DAY');
INSERT INTO test_dates (id, event_date) VALUES (3, CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '1 DAY');
SELECT id, event_date FROM test_dates WHERE event_date = CURRENT_DATE;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_dates WHERE event_date < CURRENT_DATE;
SELECT id FROM test_dates WHERE event_date = (SELECT CURRENT_DATE);
SELECT id, event_date FROM test_dates WHERE event_date BETWEEN CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '7 DAYS' AND CURRENT_DATE;
SELECT id, event_date FROM test_dates WHERE event_date > (SELECT CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 DAY') AND event_date < CURRENT_DATE;
CREATE TABLE event_log (log_id INT PRIMARY KEY, log_date DATE DEFAULT CURRENT_DATE, description TEXT);
INSERT INTO event_log (log_id, description) VALUES (2, 'Event 2 with date ' || CURRENT_DATE);
SELECT log_id, log_date FROM event_log WHERE log_date = CURRENT_DATE;
SELECT e.id, COUNT(e.event_date) FROM test_dates e JOIN event_log l ON e.event_date = l.log_date GROUP BY e.id;
SELECT id, event_date FROM test_dates WHERE event_date < (SELECT CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '1 MONTH');
SELECT CURRENT_DATE, event_date FROM test_dates WHERE event_date IS NOT NULL ORDER BY event_date DESC;
